title: How Quince is Defying the E-Commerce Curse with Affordable Luxury
content:
Scrutinize a receipt from any recent purchase, and it might be hard to avoid feeling you got swindled. Since early 2020, the consumer price index has risen so quickly that everyday items have become noticeably more expensive. In some categories, including groceries, electricity, and car insurance, the price increases have been even steeper. Consequently, major retailers are seeing more affluent customers raid their aisles in search of cheaper products. While some price hikes are clearly linked to tariffs or shortages, many others seem inexplicably high to the average consumer.

When the online retailer Quince launched in 2019, the company couldn’t have anticipated the impending years of pricing chaos. However, this turbulent economic environment ultimately made for splendid timing for their specific business model. The San Francisco-based company specializes in selling its own brand of low-cost luxury goods. They offer items designed to lessen the tension between consumer aspirations and tightening budgets. Quince promises to cut out the middlemen, enabling it to sell high-quality goods at significantly lower prices.

Over the past few years, Quince has expanded into a surprising variety of other premium product categories. They now offer everything from American-made sofas and fine jewelry to fragrances and nutritional supplements. The company has built a highly efficient system predicated on sweeping data analysis and close relationships with manufacturers. These advantages have enabled the retailer to enter new product categories far more quickly than traditional models allow. Recently, Quince has gone even further by selling third-party products from recognized high-end brands to boost its overall luxury aura.
